title: 10 best herbal tinctures for phlegm
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for phlegm are concentrated liquid extracts made from herbs that have expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. They help with phlegm by reducing its production, thinning it out, and making it easier to expel from the body. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with phlegm include licorice root, wild cherry bark, elecampane, and thyme. These herbs have been used traditionally to soothe irritated throats, reduce coughing, and alleviate chest congestion. By using herbal tinctures for phlegm, individuals can experience improved respiratory function, reduced symptoms of respiratory infections, and a greater sense of well-being. For instance, a person with chronic bronchitis may find relief by taking a licorice root tincture, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated airways.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with phlegm because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that work to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and break down excess mucus. Its active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, help to soothe irritation and promote the expulsion of phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, yarrow tincture has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help to fight off infections that may be causing phlegm production, providing further relief for respiratory symptoms. Overall, yarrow tincture is a natural and effective way to alleviate phlegm and support respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Yarrow tincture helps to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel. This property is important for phlegm because it reduces congestion and inflammation in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help to alleviate symptoms of phlegm such as coughing and congestion. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to reduce irritation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and alkaloids.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections and phlegm. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to prevent the underlying cause of phlegm and reduce the risk of further infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture has astringent properties, which can help to tighten and dry up excess mucus in the respiratory tract. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to reduce the production of phlegm and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and volatile oils.

Diaphoretic: Yarrow tincture promotes sweating, which can help to eliminate toxins and reduce fever. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to eliminate the underlying causes of phlegm, such as infection and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, such as azulene and chamazulene.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, flowers help reduce inflammation and have an expectorant effect, making them useful for treating phlegm.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds, which help alleviate congestion and soothe respiratory irritation.
Stems: High in essential oils, stems aid in breaking up mucus and have a calming effect on the respiratory system.
Roots: Packed with antioxidants and tannins, roots help soothe irritated throat tissue and reduce phlegm production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, chop finely, and place in a clean jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for phlegm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with phlegm because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a slick gel when mixed with water. This gel coats the throat and soothes irritation, reducing the urge to cough and clear phlegm. Additionally, marshmallow tincture is known to have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Overall, marshmallow tincture can provide relief for individuals experiencing excessive phlegm and discomfort in the throat.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tincture creates a soothing film over mucous membranes, helping to relieve irritation and inflammation, which is important for phlegm as it reduces coughing and expectoration of thick mucus. This property is due to the high mucilage content in marshmallow root.

Expectorant: Marshmallow tincture helps to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and relieve congestion. This is attributed to the high mucilage content and polysaccharides that stimulate the release of mucus from respiratory tissue.

Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of phlegm, such as coughing and difficulty breathing. This is due to the flavonoid content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties.

Antitussive: Marshmallow tincture helps to suppress coughing, which is important for phlegm as it can reduce the irritation and inflammation that can exacerbate phlegm production. This property is due to the mucilage content, which forms a protective layer over the mucous membranes and soothes the cough reflex.

Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and viruses that can contribute to phlegm production. This is due to the presence of tannins and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The marshmallow root contains mucilage, which helps soothe irritated throats and reduce phlegm.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant also contain mucilage, making them effective in relieving congestion and reducing the production of phlegm.
Flowers: Marshmallow flowers have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of phlegm.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, which can help soothe irritated membranes and reduce the production of phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried marshmallow root and steep in 2 cups of boiling water for 20 minutes to extract the mucilaginous properties.
Strain the liquid, squeezing out the excess liquid from the marshmallow root, and compost the solids.
Combine the marshmallow root tea with 2 cups of a 50/50 mixture of vodka and vegetable glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the finished tincture in a labeled glass bottle in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tincture helps with phlegm because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes mucous membranes, making it effective in reducing phlegm and easing congestion in the respiratory tract. The herb also has expectorant properties that help loosen and expel phlegm from the lungs, providing relief from coughs and chest congestion. Additionally, great mullein has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory system, further supporting its use in managing phlegm and respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Great mullein tincture helps loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract due to its saponin content, which creates a gentle, cleansing foam.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture reduces inflammation in the respiratory system, alleviating irritation and swelling, thanks to its flavonoid and iridoid glycoside constituents.
Anticatarrhal: Great mullein tincture inhibits the production of excess mucus, reducing the amount of phlegm in the respiratory tract, by means of its verbascoside and rosmarinic acid content.
Soothing: The tincture soothes irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from persistent coughing and sore throats, owing to its demulcent mucilage content.

Antimicrobial: Great mullein tincture combats bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate phlegm production, as it contains verbascoside, rosmarinic acid, and other phenolic acids with antimicrobial properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of great mullein contain saponins and mucilage, which help soothe irritated throats and reduce phlegm.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in antioxidants and have expectorant properties, making them useful for breaking up phlegm and reducing inflammation.
Buds: The buds contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, aiding in the reduction of phlegm and inflammation.
Roots: The roots of great mullein contain various compounds, including verbascoside, that have expectorant properties, helping to expel phlegm from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves of the mullein plant, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides, then rinse and dry them.
Chop or crush 2 ounces of dried mullein leaves and place them in a clean, dry jar, filling it 3/4 full.
Pour 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol until the jar is completely filled, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material. Store the finished mullein tincture in a dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip tincture helps with phlegm because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. This action helps to reduce the production of phlegm, making it easier to expel. Additionally, cowslip has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs. Its anti-inflammatory and calming effects can also help to alleviate symptoms associated with phlegm, such as coughing and congestion. Overall, cowslip tincture can provide relief for individuals suffering from respiratory conditions that involve phlegm, such as bronchitis, asthma, and the common cold.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Cowslip tincture helps to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel. This property is important for phlegm because it helps to reduce congestion and alleviate breathing difficulties. The expectorant effect of cowslip tincture is attributed to its mucilaginous and saponin content.

Anti-inflammatory: Cowslip tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms associated with phlegm. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to reduce irritation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The anti-inflammatory effect of cowslip tincture is attributed to its flavonoid content.

Antispasmodic: Cowslip tincture has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract and alleviate coughing and wheezing. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits, making it easier to expel phlegm. The antispasmodic effect of cowslip tincture is attributed to its alkaloid content.

Antimicrobial: Cowslip tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and viruses that can contribute to phlegm. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to reduce the severity and duration of respiratory infections. The antimicrobial effect of cowslip tincture is attributed to its saponin and tannin content.

Demulcent: Cowslip tincture has demulcent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to reduce irritation and inflammation, making it easier to expel phlegm. The demulcent effect of cowslip tincture is attributed to its mucilaginous content.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in mucilage, cowslip flowers help soothe irritated throats and loosen phlegm.
Leaves: Containing saponins, cowslip leaves can help eliminate excess mucus and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Packed with antioxidants, cowslip roots can help alleviate congestion and support respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cowslip flowers in early spring, choosing fresh and bright blooms.
Fill a clean jar with 2/3 of dried, finely chopped cowslip flowers and add 1/3 of 80-proof vodka or rum.
Let the mixture steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every other day.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for phlegm relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound tincture helps with phlegm because it contains active compounds that act as an expectorant, stimulating the cough reflex and promoting the loosening and expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, horehound has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat any bacterial or viral infections that may be contributing to phlegm production. By combining these actions, horehound tincture can provide relief for those experiencing congestion, coughing, and other symptoms associated with excess phlegm.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Horehound tincture helps to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract by stimulating the production of respiratory secretions, due to its active constituents, diterpene bitters such as marrubiin.
Antitussive: It helps to suppress a persistent cough by reducing the cough reflex, thanks to its diterpene bitters and volatile oils.
Anti-inflammatory: Horehound tincture can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation and discomfort caused by phlegm, owing to its flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: It may help to fight off respiratory infections that can cause phlegm accumulation, as a result of its volatile oils and diterpene bitters.

Diaphoretic: Horehound tincture promotes sweating, which can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fevers, contributing to the overall improvement of respiratory health and the reduction of phlegm, due to its volatile oils and diterpene bitters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in flavonoids and diterpenes, horehound leaves are often used to make medicinal tea for phlegm due to their expectorant and soothing properties.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, horehound flowers can help reduce inflammation and encourage the expulsion of phlegm when used in herbal teas.
Roots: Containing marrubiin and other bitter compounds, horehound roots are used in teas to stimulate mucus production and help clear congested airways.
Seeds: Packed with beneficial oils and nutrients, horehound seeds can help alleviate phlegm by promoting the expulsion of mucus and soothing irritated respiratory tracts.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried horehound herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and transfer the liquid to a clean, dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, ingredients, and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for phlegm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tincture helps with phlegm because it contains saponins, which have expectorant properties that can help loosen and break up mucus in the respiratory tract. This herbal remedy has been used traditionally to alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The tincture works by stimulating the release of surfactant, a substance that reduces surface tension in the lungs and makes it easier to expel phlegm. By thinning and expelling mucus, English ivy tincture can help improve breathing and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: English ivy tincture helps to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel mucus. This property is important for phlegm because it helps to clear the airways and reduce congestion, allowing for easier breathing. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of phlegm such as coughing and congestion. This property is important for phlegm because inflammation can exacerbate phlegm production and make it more difficult to expel. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: English ivy tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections and exacerbate phlegm production. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to prevent and treat infections that can cause or worsen phlegm. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids.

Bronchodilator: English ivy tincture can help to dilate the bronchioles in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to alleviate symptoms of phlegm such as coughing and congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids.

Antitussive: English ivy tincture has antitussive properties that can help to suppress coughing, which can help to alleviate symptoms of phlegm. This property is important for phlegm because coughing can exacerbate phlegm production and make it more difficult to expel. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy plant contain saponins, which help to break down and expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Stems: The stems of the English ivy plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and swelling in the throat.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy plant contain polyphenols, which have expectorant properties that can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs.
Bark: The bark of the English ivy plant contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh English ivy leaves and stems (1 cup) and combine with 2 cups of a 50/50 mixture of vodka and water in a clean jar.
Securely seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
Take 1-2 mL of the tincture up to three times a day for phlegm relief, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with phlegm because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats and soothes mucous membranes, making it effective in reducing excess phlegm in the respiratory tract. Its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties further aid in breaking down and expelling mucus, providing relief to individuals suffering from congestion, coughs, and other respiratory conditions. The herb's demulcent qualities also help to reduce irritation in the throat and lungs, creating a soothing effect on the user. Overall, plantain tincture is a valuable natural remedy for managing phlegm and promoting respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Plantain tincture helps loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract due to its mucilaginous and demulcent properties, which soothe irritated mucous membranes. The active constituents responsible for this effect are polysaccharides and tannins found in the plant.

Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation and swelling that can exacerbate phlegm production. This is attributed to the plant's flavonoid content, such as luteolin and apigenin.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause or worsen phlegm, supporting the immune system in fighting off respiratory infections. This is due to the presence of compounds like aucubin, catalpol, and tannins.

Antitussive: Plantain tincture helps suppress coughs associated with phlegm by soothing the throat and reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. The mucilaginous and demulcent properties of polysaccharides in the plant contribute to this effect.

Antihistamine: Plantain tincture may help alleviate allergic reactions that can lead to increased phlegm production. This is because the plant contains compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which have natural antihistamine properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in mucilage, leaves help soothe irritated throats and reduce phlegm.
Seeds: Rich in fiber, seeds aid in the expulsion of phlegm and promote a healthier respiratory system.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, flowers help alleviate congestion and reduce phlegm production.
Bark: Packed with antioxidants, bark supports the immune system and assists in managing phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves (Plantago major or lanceolata) from a clean, pesticide-free source, picking the healthiest, mature leaves.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then chop or tear them into small pieces and fill a clean, dry jar, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Pour 80-proof vodka (or another food-grade menstruum) over the plantain leaves, ensuring they are completely submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After the maceration period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent plant material. Store the finished plantain tincture in a dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight. For phlegm, consider a dosage of 2-4 ml, up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian tobacco tincture helps with phlegm because it contains natural expectorant properties that aid in breaking down and expelling mucus from the respiratory tract. The herbal ingredients in the tincture, such as Lobelia inflata, work to stimulate the cough reflex and relax the bronchial muscles, making it easier to expel phlegm. Additionally, the tincture has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe irritated throat and lungs, reducing the production of phlegm and providing relief from respiratory symptoms. Overall, the use of Indian tobacco tincture can help promote healthy respiratory function and alleviate phlegm-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Indian tobacco tincture helps to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel. This property is important for phlegm because it helps to clear airways and reduce congestion, allowing for easier breathing. The active constituents that give Indian tobacco tincture its expectorant properties include Lobeline and Nicotine.

Anti-inflammatory: Indian tobacco tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help to alleviate symptoms of phlegm such as coughing and congestion. This property is important for phlegm because inflammation can exacerbate phlegm production and make it more difficult to expel. The active constituents that give Indian tobacco tincture its anti-inflammatory properties include Lobeline and Phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Indian tobacco tincture has antimicrobial properties that help to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections and exacerbate phlegm production. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to prevent or treat infections that can lead to excessive phlegm production. The active constituents that give Indian tobacco tincture its antimicrobial properties include Lobeline and Nicotine.

Analgesic: Indian tobacco tincture has analgesic properties that help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with phlegm, such as coughing and chest tightness. This property is important for phlegm because it can help to alleviate symptoms and improve comfort. The active constituents that give Indian tobacco tincture its analgesic properties include Lobeline and Nicotine.

Bronchodilator: Indian tobacco tincture has bronchodilator properties that help to widen the airways and improve airflow to the lungs, which can help to alleviate symptoms of phlegm such as coughing and congestion. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Indian tobacco tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for phlegm due to their expectorant properties that help clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are rich in saponins, which have been shown to have expectorant and soothing effects on the respiratory system, making them useful for treating phlegm.
Flowers: The flowers of the Indian tobacco tincture plant contain mucilage, which helps to soothe irritated membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing the production of phlegm.
Seeds: The seeds of the plant contain essential oils that have expectorant and antispasmodic properties, making them useful for treating phlegm and cough.
Bark: The bark of the Indian tobacco tincture plant contains alkaloids that have expectorant and antitussive properties, making it useful for treating phlegm and cough.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried Indian tobacco leaves and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) to the jar, ensuring the leaves are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the ingredients, date, and dosage recommendations (typically 1-2 mL up to three times a day), and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tincture helps with phlegm because it contains compounds that have expectorant and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in soothing coughs and breaking down mucus. Thymol, one of the main active components in thyme, has been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and fight respiratory infections. Furthermore, thyme has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and whooping cough. Therefore, thyme tincture can provide relief from phlegm and congestion, making it easier to breathe and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Thyme tincture helps loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract by stimulating mucus secretion, making it easier to cough up and expel the phlegm. This property is important for phlegm because it helps reduce congestion and alleviate breathing difficulties. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol.

Antispasmodic: Thyme tincture can help relax the muscles of the trachea and bronchi, reducing the coughing reflex and providing relief from persistent, dry coughs. This property is important for phlegm because it helps alleviate the constant need to cough, which can be exhausting and painful. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tincture has potent antimicrobial properties that help eliminate the bacteria and viruses responsible for respiratory infections, which can cause phlegm production. This property is important for phlegm because it helps treat the underlying cause of phlegm and prevents its recurrence. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol, as well as flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort. This property is important for phlegm because it helps reduce swelling in the airways, facilitating the expulsion of phlegm and improving overall respiratory health. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.

Mucolytic: Thyme tincture can help break down and thin out thick, stubborn mucus, making it easier to expel from the respiratory tract. This property is important for phlegm because it helps reduce the viscosity of phlegm, allowing for easier expectoration and reducing the risk of respiratory infections. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which help to loosen phlegm and have an expectorant effect.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and bioactive compounds, which have antispasmodic and expectorant properties, making them useful for relieving phlegm.
Stems: Thyme stems contain beneficial compounds, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, aiding in the elimination of phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried thyme herb, place in a clean jar, and fill the jar with 8 ounces of 100-proof vodka.
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dry jar or bottle, squeezing out all liquid from the thyme.
Discard the used thyme and store the strained liquid in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
To use, dilute 1-2 mL of the thyme tincture in water and take up to three times a day as needed for phlegm relief.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, the flowers of eastern purple coneflower are used to make medicinal tea that helps break up phlegm and reduce inflammation.

Roots: Packed with phenolic acids and tannins, the roots of eastern purple coneflower are used in medicinal tea for phlegm to provide anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests or diseases.
Chop or grind the herbs to increase their surface area, placing them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 100-proof vodka or another food-grade alcohol until the herbs are completely submerged, then securely fasten a lid on the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day to help the extraction process.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat phlegm with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of expectorant and anti-inflammatory herbs. Expectorant herbs, such as licorice, elecampane, and wild cherry bark, help loosen and expel phlegm from the lungs. Anti-inflammatory herbs, like turmeric and ginger, reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and discomfort. A tincture blend of these herbs, taken three times a day, will provide relief and support the body's natural ability to heal. Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and suitability.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to phlegm that are treated with herbal tinctures include respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). These tinctures often contain expectorant and anti-inflammatory herbs like licorice, wild cherry bark, and elecampane, which help to thin and expel mucus, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritated tissues. Other herbs like yerba santa and horehound may also be used for their mucolytic and antitussive properties to alleviate symptoms associated with phlegm and improve overall respiratory health.
